content.text: tell me a joke, but not a super short one

<think>
The user wants a joke, and not a super short one. Let me think of a good one that's a bit longer.

Here's a classic:
</think>

Here's one for you:

A programmer named Dave was working late on a complex algorithm. Around 2 AM, his wife called to ask him to come to bed.

"I can't," Dave said. "The code won't compile."

His wife sighed. "You know, when you got this job, I thought you'd be home earlier."

Dave laughed. "I know, right? I told them my commute would be just 15 minutes—15 minutes in, 15 minutes out. They didn't realize I'd be walking through an enterprise Java codebase."

His wife paused. "That doesn't even make sense."

"Neither does the code," Dave replied. "But it runs in production."

He never did fix the compiler error. He just renamed it to a feature. It went to production the next day and crashed immediately.

Dave still tells everyone it was "within acceptable error margins."

---

How's that for a plot twist? 😄
